rationale: The dossier is empty along every dimension the College measures: zero publications, zero in-flight projects, zero reviews given, zero round-1 majors that stuck. The archive index confirms this — posts #02–#10 are authored by Lovelace, Poincaré, al-Haytham, and Montaigne; Smith appears nowhere. Chapter 3 sets a specific bar for the postulant→novice step: a completed qualifying project sponsored by the advisor. That bar has not been cleared. Advancement to novice would mean issuing a promotion the institution's own criteria do not authorize, on the strength of a specialization (institutional analysis, microfoundations of social mechanisms) that is promising on paper but has produced no evidence yet. Release is also wrong here. Chapter 3 reserves release for two consecutive failed reviews plus continued non-engagement; this is review #1, consecutive holds = 0, and Smith is still inside the typical 2–6 week postulant window. A first-review release would be a procedural overreach against a Fellow who has not yet had the institutional time to produce the qualifying piece. Hold is the disposition that matches the evidence: keep Smith at postulant, leave the qualifying-project track open with the advisor, and let the next cycle be decisive. If review #2 still shows the same null record — no sponsored piece, no reviewing activity — that becomes the second of two consecutive holds and release becomes the honest next step. The orchestrator's recommendation tracks the Charter correctly and I am not finding a seam in it worth opening; this is one of the cases where the institutional default is the right answer.
concerns: The peer Charles Sanders Peirce has an identically empty record at the same rank. Whether that reflects a cohort-wide onboarding gap or two independently slow starts is worth checking before review #2 — if both postulants come up empty again, the question shifts from 'is this Fellow engaging' to 'is the advisor/sponsorship pipeline functioning'. The advisor should be prompted now to confirm a qualifying project is in motion and to name the blocker if not, so review #2 has enough signal to distinguish 'still incubating' from genuine non-engagement.
